tags.name: The Postal Museum
tags.wheelchair:description: Slight ramp from street, wide automatic door. Museum is on a single level. Lift to 1st floor discovery centre. Accessible WC without RADAR key. Step-free to Mail Rail.

tags.name: Mail Rail Museum
tags.wheelchair:description: RADAR key Changing Places toilet not checked today. Lift (reverse in/out, no mirror) down to exhibition area with wide level aisles. Street entrance has automatic sliding door.

tags.name: British Optical Association Museum
tags.note: Open weekdays, by appointment only (also may be open on Open House London weekend).

tags.description: This box is a non functioning red phone box now run by a charity. and restored to how it would look in the 1940s
tags.name: Restored K6 phone booth

tags.note: website: "Please note the UCL Art Museum is closed to visitors until further notice due to preparations ahead of our Bicentennial year in 2026." (as at 6 February 2025)

tags.description: The Royal Academy of Arts is one of Britain's foremost cultural institutions and a major attraction located in the heart of Piccadilly. Established in 1768 and run to this day by eminent artists, the Academy is home to a world renowned exhibition program.
tags.name: Royal Academy of Arts

tags.description: Formerly the home of neo-classical architect John Soane. It holds many drawings and architectural models of Soane's projects and a large collection of paintings, sculptures, drawings, and antiquities that he acquired over many years.
tags.name: Sir John Soane's Museum
tags.name:ja: ジョン・ソーン博物館

tags.fixme: Check the split between this (which is marked museum on OS) and the gallery to the south (marked Acad on OS).
